title: Vocabulário Essencial
description: 35 palavras de alta frequência do dia a dia que brasileiros costumam não conhecer ou confundir.

cards.example: My schedule is full this week.
I have a dentist appointment at 3 p.m.
The deadline for the report is Friday.
The concert last night was awesome.
There was an awkward silence after his comment.
Can I borrow your pen for a second?
She lent me her umbrella.

cards.back: lembrar alguém de algo (fazer lembrar; remember = lembrar-se)
cards.example: Remind me to call the bank tomorrow.

cards.back: lembrar-se (você mesmo; remind = fazer alguém lembrar)
cards.example: I don't remember his name.
We need to fix this issue before launch.
Are you available for a call this afternoon?
Sorry, I'm busy right now — can I call you back?
The subway was really crowded this morning.
She's the most reliable person on the team.
He was upset about losing the game.
I'm tired of waiting in line.

cards.back: com fome (em inglês você 'está' faminto: I'm hungry)
cards.example: I'm hungry — let's grab something to eat.

cards.back: clima, tempo (meteorológico; não confundir com time = tempo/horas)
cards.example: The weather is beautiful today.
I was late because of the traffic.
We live in a quiet neighborhood.
The landlord raised the rent again.
Could we get the bill, please?
Keep the change.
They gave me a 20% discount on the shoes.

cards.back: barato (pode soar 'de má qualidade'; inexpensive é mais neutro)
cards.example: This restaurant is really cheap and the food is great.
That hotel is too expensive for us.
The meeting starts at nine sharp.
I'm having lunch with a coworker today.
My boss approved my vacation request.
She asked her boss for a raise.
My luggage got lost at the airport.
Our flight was delayed by two hours.
I bought two tickets for the show.

cards.back: conselho (incontável: a piece of advice; o verbo é advise)
cards.example: Let me give you a piece of advice.
Please keep your belongings with you at all times.
